# Python code to calculate churn rate from telecom customer data in Excel

Python in Excel can calculate telecom churn rates using =PY() on customer data, revealing pricing insights like high charges driving 80%+ churn in short-tenure deals. This helps adjust prices for retention while hitting margins, using your Kaggle-style datasets.[^1][^2]

## Churn Calculation Code

Paste into Excel: =PY(

```
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Data (xl("A1:H20", headers=True) for your sheet)
data = {
    'tenure_months': [12,1,24,3,36,6,18,2,48,9],
    'monthly_charges': [29.99,85.20,45.50,110.00,35.75,95.00,55.25,78.50,42.00,65.00],
    'contract': ['Month-to-month','Two year','One year','Month-to-month','Two year','Month-to-month','One year','Month-to-month','Two year','One year'],
    'churn': ['Yes','No','No','Yes','No','Yes','No','Yes','No','Yes']  # 'Yes'/'No'
}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df['churn_binary'] = (df['churn'] == 'Yes').astype(int)

# Key metrics
overall_churn = df['churn_binary'].mean()
churn_by_contract = df.groupby('contract')['churn_binary'].mean()
high_churn = df[df['monthly_charges'] > 70]['churn_binary'].mean()

# Buckets for pricing strategy
df['tenure_bucket'] = pd.cut(df['tenure_months'], [0,6,12,24,50], labels=['0-6m','6-12m','12-24m','24+m'])
churn_by_tenure = df.groupby('tenure_bucket')['churn_binary'].mean()

pd.concat([pd.DataFrame({'Overall': [overall_churn], 'High Charges (>70)': [high_churn]}),
           churn_by_contract.to_frame('By Contract')], axis=1).round(3)
```

Outputs churn breakdowns for pricing decisions, e.g., avoid high prices on month-to-month.

## Sample Results

| Metric | Rate |
| :-- | :-- |
| Overall Churn Rate | 0.500 |
| Month-to-month | 1.000 |
| One year | 0.333 |
| Two year | 0.000 |

Avg monthly charges: Churn customers €75.70 vs. retained €52.74—price lower for risky segments.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Download . Extend with your costs for "safe" pricing simulations.[^3]
